variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.PerformanceDC
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.__init__
documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_enabled
documentation:
Enables/Disables the driver chip. The driver parameters can be configured
(velocity, acceleration, etc) before it is enabled.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_enabled
documentation: Returns *true* if the driver chip is enabled, *false* otherwise.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_velocity
documentation:
Sets the velocity of the motor. Whereas -32767 is full speed backward,
0 is stop and 32767 is full speed forward. Depending on the
acceleration (see {func}`Set Motion`), the motor is not immediately
brought to the velocity but smoothly accelerated.

The velocity describes the duty cycle of the PWM with which the motor is
controlled, e.g. a velocity of 3277 sets a PWM with a 10% duty cycle.
You can not only control the duty cycle of the PWM but also the frequency,
see {func}`Set PWM Frequency`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_velocity
documentation: Returns the velocity as set by {func}`Set Velocity`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_current_velocity
documentation:
Returns the *current* velocity of the motor. This value is different
from {func}`Get Velocity` whenever the motor is currently accelerating
to a goal set by {func}`Set Velocity`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_motion
documentation:
Sets the acceleration and deceleration of the motor. It is given in *velocity/s*.
An acceleration of 10000 means, that every second the velocity is increased
by 10000 (or about 30% duty cycle).

For example: If the current velocity is 0 and you want to accelerate to a
velocity of 16000 (about 50% duty cycle) in 10 seconds, you should set
an acceleration of 1600.

If acceleration and deceleration is set to 0, there is no speed ramping, i.e. a
new velocity is immediately given to the motor.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_motion
documentation: Returns the acceleration/deceleration as set by {func}`Set Motion`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.full_brake
documentation:
Executes an active full brake.

:::{warning}
This function is for emergency purposes,
where an immediate brake is necessary. Depending on the current velocity and
the strength of the motor, a full brake can be quite violent.
:::

Call {func}`Set Velocity` with 0 if you just want to stop the motor.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_drive_mode
documentation:
Sets the drive mode. Possible modes are:

* 0 = Drive/Brake
* 1 = Drive/Coast

These modes are different kinds of motor controls.

In Drive/Brake mode, the motor is always either driving or braking. There
is no freewheeling. Advantages are: A more linear correlation between
PWM and velocity, more exact accelerations and the possibility to drive
with slower velocities.

In Drive/Coast mode, the motor is always either driving or freewheeling.
Advantages are: Less current consumption and less demands on the motor and
driver chip.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_drive_mode
documentation: Returns the drive mode, as set by {func}`Set Drive Mode`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_pwm_frequency
documentation:
Sets the frequency of the PWM with which the motor is driven.
Often a high frequency
is less noisy and the motor runs smoother. However, with a low frequency
there are less switches and therefore fewer switching losses. Also with
most motors lower frequencies enable higher torque.

If you have no idea what all this means, just ignore this function and use
the default frequency, it will very likely work fine.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_pwm_frequency
documentation: Returns the PWM frequency as set by {func}`Set PWM Frequency`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_power_statistics
documentation: Returns input voltage, current usage and temperature of the driver.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_thermal_shutdown
documentation:
Sets a temperature threshold that is used for thermal shutdown.

Additionally to this user defined threshold the driver chip will shut down at a
temperature of 150°C.

If a thermal shutdown is triggered the driver is disabled and has to be
explicitly re-enabled with {func}`Set Enabled`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_thermal_shutdown
documentation: Returns the thermal shutdown temperature as set by {func}`Set Thermal Shutdown`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_gpio_configuration
documentation:
Sets the GPIO configuration for the given channel.
You can configure a debounce and the deceleration that is used if the action is
configured as `normal stop`. See {func}`Set GPIO Action`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_gpio_configuration
documentation: Returns the GPIO configuration for a channel as set by {func}`Set GPIO Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_gpio_action
documentation:
Sets the GPIO action for the given channel.

The action can be a normal stop, a full brake or a callback. Each for a rising
edge or falling edge. The actions are a bitmask they can be used at the same time.
You can for example trigger a full brake and a callback at the same time or for
rising and falling edge.

The deceleration speed for the normal stop can be configured with
{func}`Set GPIO Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_gpio_action
documentation: Returns the GPIO action for a channel as set by {func}`Set GPIO Action`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_gpio_state
documentation:
Returns the GPIO state for both channels. True if the state is `high` and
false if the state is `low`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_error_led_config
documentation:
Configures the error LED to be either turned off, turned on, blink in
heartbeat mode or show an error.

If the LED is configured to show errors it has three different states:

* Off: No error present.
* 1s interval blinking: Input voltage too low (below 6V).
* 250ms interval blinking: Overtemperature or overcurrent.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_error_led_config
documentation: Returns the LED configuration as set by {func}`Set Error LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_cw_led_config
documentation:
Configures the CW LED to be either turned off, turned on, blink in
heartbeat mode or if the motor turn clockwise.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_cw_led_config
documentation: Returns the LED configuration as set by {func}`Set CW LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_ccw_led_config
documentation:
Configures the CCW LED to be either turned off, turned on, blink in
heartbeat mode or if the motor turn counter-clockwise.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_ccw_led_config
documentation: Returns the LED configuration as set by {func}`Set CCW LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_gpio_led_config
documentation:
Configures the GPIO LED to be either turned off, turned on, blink in
heartbeat mode or the GPIO state.

The GPIO LED can be configured for both channels.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_gpio_led_config
documentation: Returns the LED configuration as set by {func}`Set GPIO LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_emergency_shutdown_callback_configuration
documentation: Enable/Disable {cb}`Emergency Shutdown` callback.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_emergency_shutdown_callback_configuration
documentation:
Returns the callback configuration as set by
{func}`Set Emergency Shutdown Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_velocity_reached_callback_configuration
documentation: Enable/Disable {cb}`Velocity Reached` callback.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_velocity_reached_callback_configuration
documentation:
Returns the callback configuration as set by
{func}`Set Velocity Reached Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_current_velocity_callback_configuration
documentation:
The period is the period with which the {cb}`Current Velocity`
callback is triggered periodically. A value of 0 turns the callback off.

If the `value has to change`-parameter is set to true, the callback is only
triggered after the value has changed. If the value didn't change within the
period, the callback is triggered immediately on change.

If it is set to false, the callback is continuously triggered with the period,
independent of the value.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_current_velocity_callback_configuration
documentation:
Returns the callback configuration as set by
{func}`Set Current Velocity Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_spitfp_error_count
documentation:
Returns the error count for the communication between Brick and Bricklet.

The errors are divided into

* ACK checksum errors,
* message checksum errors,
* framing errors and
* overflow errors.

The errors counts are for errors that occur on the Bricklet side. All
Bricks have a similar function that returns the errors on the Brick side.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_bootloader_mode
documentation:
Sets the bootloader mode and returns the status after the requested
mode change was instigated.

You can change from bootloader mode to firmware mode and vice versa. A change
from bootloader mode to firmware mode will only take place if the entry function,
device identifier and CRC are present and correct.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_bootloader_mode
documentation: Returns the current bootloader mode, see {func}`Set Bootloader Mode`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_write_firmware_pointer
documentation:
Sets the firmware pointer for {func}`Write Firmware`. The pointer has
to be increased by chunks of size 64. The data is written to flash
every 4 chunks (which equals to one page of size 256).

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.write_firmware
documentation:
Writes 64 Bytes of firmware at the position as written by
{func}`Set Write Firmware Pointer` before. The firmware is written
to flash every 4 chunks.

You can only write firmware in bootloader mode.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.set_status_led_config
documentation:
Sets the status LED configuration. By default the LED shows
communication traffic between Brick and Bricklet, it flickers once
for every 10 received data packets.

You can also turn the LED permanently on/off or show a heartbeat.

If the Bricklet is in bootloader mode, the LED is will show heartbeat by default.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_status_led_config
documentation: Returns the configuration as set by {func}`Set Status LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_chip_temperature
documentation:
Returns the temperature as measured inside the microcontroller. The
value returned is not the ambient temperature!

The temperature is only proportional to the real temperature and it has bad
accuracy. Practically it is only useful as an indicator for
temperature changes.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.reset
documentation:
Calling this function will reset the Bricklet. All configurations
will be lost.

After a reset you have to create new device objects,
calling functions on the existing ones will result in
undefined behavior!

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.write_uid
documentation:
Writes a new UID into flash. If you want to set a new UID
you have to decode the Base58 encoded UID string into an
integer first.

We recommend that you use Brick Viewer to change the UID.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.read_uid
documentation:
Returns the current UID as an integer. Encode as
Base58 to get the usual string version.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.get_identity
documentation:
Returns the UID, the UID where the Bricklet is connected to,
the position, the hardware and firmware version as well as the
device identifier.

The position can be 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', 'g' or 'h' (Bricklet Port).
A Bricklet connected to an {ref}`Isolator Bricklet <isolator_bricklet>` is always at
position 'z'.

The device identifier numbers can be found {ref}`here <device_identifier>`.
{{ device_identifier_constant }}

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_performance_dc.BrickletPerformanceDC.register_callback
documentation: Registers the given *function* with the given *callback_id*.

Drives one brushed DC motor with up to 36V and 10A

documentation: -*- coding: utf-8 -*-

variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.MotorizedLinearPoti
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.__init__
documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_position
documentation:
Returns the position of the linear potentiometer. The value is
between 0 (slider down) and 100 (slider up).

If you want to get the value periodically, it is recommended to use the
{cb}`Position` callback. You can set the callback configuration
with {func}`Set Position Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.set_position_callback_configuration
documentation:
The period is the period with which the {cb}`Position` callback is triggered
periodically. A value of 0 turns the callback off.

If the `value has to change`-parameter is set to true, the callback is only
triggered after the value has changed. If the value didn't change
within the period, the callback is triggered immediately on change.

If it is set to false, the callback is continuously triggered with the period,
independent of the value.

It is furthermore possible to constrain the callback with thresholds.

The `option`-parameter together with min/max sets a threshold for the {cb}`Position` callback.

The following options are possible:

```
.. csv-table::
 :header: "Option", "Description"
 :widths: 10, 100

 "'x'",    "Threshold is turned off"
 "'o'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is *outside* the min and max values"
 "'i'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is *inside* or equal to the min and max values"
 "'<'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is smaller than the min value (max is ignored)"
 "'>'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is greater than the min value (max is ignored)"
```

If the option is set to 'x' (threshold turned off) the callback is triggered with the fixed period.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_position_callback_configuration
documentation: Returns the callback configuration as set by {func}`Set Position Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.set_motor_position
documentation:
Sets the position of the potentiometer. The motorized potentiometer will
immediately start to approach the position. Depending on the chosen drive mode,
the position will either be reached as fast as possible or in a slow but smooth
motion.

The position has to be between 0 (slider down) and 100 (slider up).

If you set the hold position parameter to true, the position will automatically
be retained. If a user changes the position of the potentiometer, it will
automatically drive back to the original set point.

If the hold position parameter is set to false, the potentiometer can be changed
again by the user as soon as the set point was reached once.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_motor_position
documentation:
Returns the last motor position as set by {func}`Set Motor Position`. This is not
the current position (use {func}`Get Position` to get the current position). This
is the last used set point and configuration.

The position reached parameter is true if the position has been reached at one point.
The position may have been changed again in the meantime by the user.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.calibrate
documentation:
Starts a calibration procedure. The potentiometer will be driven to the extreme
points to calibrate the potentiometer.

The calibration is saved in flash, it does not have to be called on every start up.

The Motorized Linear Poti Bricklet is already factory-calibrated during
testing at Tinkerforge.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.set_position_reached_callback_configuration
documentation: Enables/Disables {cb}`Position Reached` callback.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_position_reached_callback_configuration
documentation:
Returns the {cb}`Position Reached` callback configuration
as set by {func}`Set Position Reached Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_spitfp_error_count
documentation:
Returns the error count for the communication between Brick and Bricklet.

The errors are divided into

* ACK checksum errors,
* message checksum errors,
* framing errors and
* overflow errors.

The errors counts are for errors that occur on the Bricklet side. All
Bricks have a similar function that returns the errors on the Brick side.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.set_bootloader_mode
documentation:
Sets the bootloader mode and returns the status after the requested
mode change was instigated.

You can change from bootloader mode to firmware mode and vice versa. A change
from bootloader mode to firmware mode will only take place if the entry function,
device identifier and CRC are present and correct.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_bootloader_mode
documentation: Returns the current bootloader mode, see {func}`Set Bootloader Mode`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.set_write_firmware_pointer
documentation:
Sets the firmware pointer for {func}`Write Firmware`. The pointer has
to be increased by chunks of size 64. The data is written to flash
every 4 chunks (which equals to one page of size 256).

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.write_firmware
documentation:
Writes 64 Bytes of firmware at the position as written by
{func}`Set Write Firmware Pointer` before. The firmware is written
to flash every 4 chunks.

You can only write firmware in bootloader mode.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.set_status_led_config
documentation:
Sets the status LED configuration. By default the LED shows
communication traffic between Brick and Bricklet, it flickers once
for every 10 received data packets.

You can also turn the LED permanently on/off or show a heartbeat.

If the Bricklet is in bootloader mode, the LED is will show heartbeat by default.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_status_led_config
documentation: Returns the configuration as set by {func}`Set Status LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_chip_temperature
documentation:
Returns the temperature as measured inside the microcontroller. The
value returned is not the ambient temperature!

The temperature is only proportional to the real temperature and it has bad
accuracy. Practically it is only useful as an indicator for
temperature changes.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.reset
documentation:
Calling this function will reset the Bricklet. All configurations
will be lost.

After a reset you have to create new device objects,
calling functions on the existing ones will result in
undefined behavior!

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.write_uid
documentation:
Writes a new UID into flash. If you want to set a new UID
you have to decode the Base58 encoded UID string into an
integer first.

We recommend that you use Brick Viewer to change the UID.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.read_uid
documentation:
Returns the current UID as an integer. Encode as
Base58 to get the usual string version.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.get_identity
documentation:
Returns the UID, the UID where the Bricklet is connected to,
the position, the hardware and firmware version as well as the
device identifier.

The position can be 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', 'g' or 'h' (Bricklet Port).
A Bricklet connected to an {ref}`Isolator Bricklet <isolator_bricklet>` is always at
position 'z'.

The device identifier numbers can be found {ref}`here <device_identifier>`.
{{ device_identifier_constant }}

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.register_callback
documentation: Registers the given *function* with the given *callback_id*.

class_variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_motorized_linear_poti.BrickletMotorizedLinearPoti.DEVICE_URL_PART
documentation: internal
Motorized Linear Potentiometer

variables.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.DC
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.__init__
documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_velocity
documentation:
Sets the velocity of the motor. Whereas -32767 is full speed backward,
0 is stop and 32767 is full speed forward. Depending on the
acceleration (see {func}`Set Acceleration`), the motor is not immediately
brought to the velocity but smoothly accelerated.

The velocity describes the duty cycle of the PWM with which the motor is
controlled, e.g. a velocity of 3277 sets a PWM with a 10% duty cycle.
You can not only control the duty cycle of the PWM but also the frequency,
see {func}`Set PWM Frequency`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_velocity
documentation: Returns the velocity as set by {func}`Set Velocity`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_current_velocity
documentation:
Returns the *current* velocity of the motor. This value is different
from {func}`Get Velocity` whenever the motor is currently accelerating
to a goal set by {func}`Set Velocity`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_acceleration
documentation:
Sets the acceleration of the motor. It is given in *velocity/s*. An
acceleration of 10000 means, that every second the velocity is increased
by 10000 (or about 30% duty cycle).

For example: If the current velocity is 0 and you want to accelerate to a
velocity of 16000 (about 50% duty cycle) in 10 seconds, you should set
an acceleration of 1600.

If acceleration is set to 0, there is no speed ramping, i.e. a new velocity
is immediately given to the motor.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_acceleration
documentation: Returns the acceleration as set by {func}`Set Acceleration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_pwm_frequency
documentation:
Sets the frequency of the PWM with which the motor is driven.
Often a high frequency
is less noisy and the motor runs smoother. However, with a low frequency
there are less switches and therefore fewer switching losses. Also with
most motors lower frequencies enable higher torque.

If you have no idea what all this means, just ignore this function and use
the default frequency, it will very likely work fine.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_pwm_frequency
documentation: Returns the PWM frequency as set by {func}`Set PWM Frequency`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.full_brake
documentation:
Executes an active full brake.

:::{warning}
This function is for emergency purposes,
where an immediate brake is necessary. Depending on the current velocity and
the strength of the motor, a full brake can be quite violent.
:::

Call {func}`Set Velocity` with 0 if you just want to stop the motor.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_stack_input_voltage
documentation:
Returns the stack input voltage. The stack input voltage is the
voltage that is supplied via the stack, i.e. it is given by a
Step-Down or Step-Up Power Supply.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_external_input_voltage
documentation:
Returns the external input voltage. The external input voltage is
given via the black power input connector on the DC Brick.

If there is an external input voltage and a stack input voltage, the motor
will be driven by the external input voltage. If there is only a stack
voltage present, the motor will be driven by this voltage.

:::{warning}
This means, if you have a high stack voltage and a low external voltage,
the motor will be driven with the low external voltage. If you then remove
the external connection, it will immediately be driven by the high
stack voltage.
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_current_consumption
documentation: Returns the current consumption of the motor.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.enable
documentation:
Enables the driver chip. The driver parameters can be configured (velocity,
acceleration, etc) before it is enabled.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.disable
documentation:
Disables the driver chip. The configurations are kept (velocity,
acceleration, etc) but the motor is not driven until it is enabled again.

:::{warning}
Disabling the driver chip while the motor is still turning can damage the
driver chip. The motor should be stopped calling {func}`Set Velocity` with 0
before disabling the motor power. The {func}`Set Velocity` function will **not**
wait until the motor is actually stopped. You have to explicitly wait for the
appropriate time after calling the {func}`Set Velocity` function before calling
the {func}`Disable` function.
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.is_enabled
documentation: Returns *true* if the driver chip is enabled, *false* otherwise.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_minimum_voltage
documentation:
Sets the minimum voltage, below which the {cb}`Under Voltage` callback
is triggered. The minimum possible value that works with the DC Brick is 6V.
You can use this function to detect the discharge of a battery that is used
to drive the motor. If you have a fixed power supply, you likely do not need
this functionality.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_minimum_voltage
documentation: Returns the minimum voltage as set by {func}`Set Minimum Voltage`

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_drive_mode
documentation:
Sets the drive mode. Possible modes are:

* 0 = Drive/Brake
* 1 = Drive/Coast

These modes are different kinds of motor controls.

In Drive/Brake mode, the motor is always either driving or braking. There
is no freewheeling. Advantages are: A more linear correlation between
PWM and velocity, more exact accelerations and the possibility to drive
with slower velocities.

In Drive/Coast mode, the motor is always either driving or freewheeling.
Advantages are: Less current consumption and less demands on the motor and
driver chip.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_drive_mode
documentation: Returns the drive mode, as set by {func}`Set Drive Mode`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_current_velocity_period
documentation:
Sets a period with which the {cb}`Current Velocity` callback is triggered.
A period of 0 turns the callback off.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_current_velocity_period
documentation: Returns the period as set by {func}`Set Current Velocity Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_spitfp_baudrate_config
documentation:
The SPITF protocol can be used with a dynamic baudrate. If the dynamic baudrate is
enabled, the Brick will try to adapt the baudrate for the communication
between Bricks and Bricklets according to the amount of data that is transferred.

The baudrate will be increased exponentially if lots of data is sent/received and
decreased linearly if little data is sent/received.

This lowers the baudrate in applications where little data is transferred (e.g.
a weather station) and increases the robustness. If there is lots of data to transfer
(e.g. Thermal Imaging Bricklet) it automatically increases the baudrate as needed.

In cases where some data has to transferred as fast as possible every few seconds
(e.g. RS485 Bricklet with a high baudrate but small payload) you may want to turn
the dynamic baudrate off to get the highest possible performance.

The maximum value of the baudrate can be set per port with the function
{func}`Set SPITFP Baudrate`. If the dynamic baudrate is disabled, the baudrate
as set by {func}`Set SPITFP Baudrate` will be used statically.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.5$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_spitfp_baudrate_config
documentation:
Returns the baudrate config, see {func}`Set SPITFP Baudrate Config`.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.5$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_send_timeout_count
documentation:
Returns the timeout count for the different communication methods.

The methods 0-2 are available for all Bricks, 3-7 only for Master Bricks.

This function is mostly used for debugging during development, in normal operation
the counters should nearly always stay at 0.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.3$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.set_spitfp_baudrate
documentation:
Sets the baudrate for a specific Bricklet port.

If you want to increase the throughput of Bricklets you can increase
the baudrate. If you get a high error count because of high
interference (see {func}`Get SPITFP Error Count`) you can decrease the
baudrate.

If the dynamic baudrate feature is enabled, the baudrate set by this
function corresponds to the maximum baudrate (see {func}`Set SPITFP Baudrate Config`).

Regulatory testing is done with the default baudrate. If CE compatibility
or similar is necessary in your applications we recommend to not change
the baudrate.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.3$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_spitfp_baudrate
documentation:
Returns the baudrate for a given Bricklet port, see {func}`Set SPITFP Baudrate`.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.3$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_spitfp_error_count
documentation:
Returns the error count for the communication between Brick and Bricklet.

The errors are divided into

- ACK checksum errors,
- message checksum errors,
- framing errors and
- overflow errors.

The errors counts are for errors that occur on the Brick side. All
Bricklets have a similar function that returns the errors on the Bricklet side.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.3$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.enable_status_led
documentation:
Enables the status LED.

The status LED is the blue LED next to the USB connector. If enabled is is
on and it flickers if data is transfered. If disabled it is always off.

The default state is enabled.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.1$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.disable_status_led
documentation:
Disables the status LED.

The status LED is the blue LED next to the USB connector. If enabled is is
on and it flickers if data is transfered. If disabled it is always off.

The default state is enabled.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.1$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.is_status_led_enabled
documentation:
Returns *true* if the status LED is enabled, *false* otherwise.

:::{versionadded} 2.3.1$nbsp;(Firmware)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_protocol1_bricklet_name
documentation:
Returns the firmware and protocol version and the name of the Bricklet for a
given port.

This functions sole purpose is to allow automatic flashing of v1.x.y Bricklet
plugins.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_chip_temperature
documentation:
Returns the temperature as measured inside the microcontroller. The
value returned is not the ambient temperature!

The temperature is only proportional to the real temperature and it has an
accuracy of ±15%. Practically it is only useful as an indicator for
temperature changes.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.reset
documentation:
Calling this function will reset the Brick. Calling this function
on a Brick inside of a stack will reset the whole stack.

After a reset you have to create new device objects,
calling functions on the existing ones will result in
undefined behavior!

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.write_bricklet_plugin
documentation:
Writes 32 bytes of firmware to the bricklet attached at the given port.
The bytes are written to the position offset * 32.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.read_bricklet_plugin
documentation:
Reads 32 bytes of firmware from the bricklet attached at the given port.
The bytes are read starting at the position offset * 32.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.get_identity
documentation:
Returns the UID, the UID where the Brick is connected to,
the position, the hardware and firmware version as well as the
device identifier.

The position is the position in the stack from '0' (bottom) to '8' (top).

The device identifier numbers can be found {ref}`here <device_identifier>`.
{{ device_identifier_constant }}

methods.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.register_callback
documentation: Registers the given *function* with the given *callback_id*.

class_variables.name: tinkerforge.brick_dc.BrickDC.DEVICE_URL_PART
documentation: internal
Drives one brushed DC motor with up to 28V and 5A (peak)

variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.RotaryPoti
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.__init__
documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_position
documentation:
Returns the position of the rotary potentiometer. The value is
between -150° (turned left) and 150° (turned right).

If you want to get the position periodically, it is recommended to use the
{cb}`Position` callback and set the period with
{func}`Set Position Callback Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_analog_value
documentation:
Returns the value as read by a 12-bit analog-to-digital converter.

:::{note}
The value returned by {func}`Get Position` is averaged over several samples
to yield less noise, while {func}`Get Analog Value` gives back raw
unfiltered analog values. The only reason to use {func}`Get Analog Value` is,
if you need the full resolution of the analog-to-digital converter.
:::

If you want the analog value periodically, it is recommended to use the
{cb}`Analog Value` callback and set the period with
{func}`Set Analog Value Callback Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.set_position_callback_period
documentation:
Sets the period with which the {cb}`Position` callback is triggered
periodically. A value of 0 turns the callback off.

The {cb}`Position` callback is only triggered if the position has changed since
the last triggering.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_position_callback_period
documentation: Returns the period as set by {func}`Set Position Callback Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.set_analog_value_callback_period
documentation:
Sets the period with which the {cb}`Analog Value` callback is triggered
periodically. A value of 0 turns the callback off.

The {cb}`Analog Value` callback is only triggered if the analog value has
changed since the last triggering.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_analog_value_callback_period
documentation: Returns the period as set by {func}`Set Analog Value Callback Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.set_position_callback_threshold
documentation:
Sets the thresholds for the {cb}`Position Reached` callback.

The following options are possible:

```
.. csv-table::
 :header: "Option", "Description"
 :widths: 10, 100

 "'x'",    "Callback is turned off"
 "'o'",    "Callback is triggered when the position is *outside* the min and max values"
 "'i'",    "Callback is triggered when the position is *inside* the min and max values"
 "'<'",    "Callback is triggered when the position is smaller than the min value (max is ignored)"
 "'>'",    "Callback is triggered when the position is greater than the min value (max is ignored)"
```

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_position_callback_threshold
documentation: Returns the threshold as set by {func}`Set Position Callback Threshold`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.set_analog_value_callback_threshold
documentation:
Sets the thresholds for the {cb}`Analog Value Reached` callback.

The following options are possible:

```
.. csv-table::
 :header: "Option", "Description"
 :widths: 10, 100

 "'x'",    "Callback is turned off"
 "'o'",    "Callback is triggered when the analog value is *outside* the min and max values"
 "'i'",    "Callback is triggered when the analog value is *inside* the min and max values"
 "'<'",    "Callback is triggered when the analog value is smaller than the min value (max is ignored)"
 "'>'",    "Callback is triggered when the analog value is greater than the min value (max is ignored)"
```

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_analog_value_callback_threshold
documentation: Returns the threshold as set by {func}`Set Analog Value Callback Threshold`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.set_debounce_period
documentation:
Sets the period with which the threshold callbacks

- {cb}`Position Reached`,
- {cb}`Analog Value Reached`

are triggered, if the thresholds

- {func}`Set Position Callback Threshold`,
- {func}`Set Analog Value Callback Threshold`

keep being reached.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_debounce_period
documentation: Returns the debounce period as set by {func}`Set Debounce Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.get_identity
documentation:
Returns the UID, the UID where the Bricklet is connected to,
the position, the hardware and firmware version as well as the
device identifier.

The position can be 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', 'g' or 'h' (Bricklet Port).
A Bricklet connected to an {ref}`Isolator Bricklet <isolator_bricklet>` is always at
position 'z'.

The device identifier numbers can be found {ref}`here <device_identifier>`.
{{ device_identifier_constant }}

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_rotary_poti.BrickletRotaryPoti.register_callback
documentation: Registers the given *function* with the given *callback_id*.

documentation: internal
300° rotary potentiometer

variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.ServoV2
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.__init__
documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_status
documentation:
Returns the status information of the Servo Bricklet 2.0.

The status includes

* for each channel if it is enabled or disabled,
* for each channel the current position,
* for each channel the current velocity,
* for each channel the current usage and
* the input voltage.

Please note that the position and the velocity is a snapshot of the
current position and velocity of the servo in motion.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_enable
documentation:
Enables a servo channel (0 to 9). If a servo is enabled, the configured position,
velocity, acceleration, etc. are applied immediately.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_enabled
documentation: Returns *true* if the specified servo channel is enabled, *false* otherwise.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_position
documentation:
Sets the position in °/100 for the specified servo channel.

The default range of the position is -9000 to 9000, but it can be specified
according to your servo with {func}`Set Degree`.

If you want to control a linear servo or RC brushless motor controller or
similar with the Servo Brick, you can also define lengths or speeds with
{func}`Set Degree`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_position
documentation: Returns the position of the specified servo channel as set by {func}`Set Position`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_current_position
documentation:
Returns the *current* position of the specified servo channel. This may not be the
value of {func}`Set Position` if the servo is currently approaching a
position goal.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_current_velocity
documentation:
Returns the *current* velocity of the specified servo channel. This may not be the
velocity specified by {func}`Set Motion Configuration`. if the servo is
currently approaching a velocity goal.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_motion_configuration
documentation:
Sets the maximum velocity of the specified servo channel in °/100s as well as
the acceleration and deceleration in °/100s²

With a velocity of 0 °/100s the position will be set immediately (no velocity).

With an acc-/deceleration of 0 °/100s² the velocity will be set immediately
(no acc-/deceleration).

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_motion_configuration
documentation: Returns the motion configuration as set by {func}`Set Motion Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_pulse_width
documentation:
Sets the minimum and maximum pulse width of the specified servo channel in µs.

Usually, servos are controlled with a
`PWM <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse-width_modulation>`__, whereby the
length of the pulse controls the position of the servo. Every servo has
different minimum and maximum pulse widths, these can be specified with
this function.

If you have a datasheet for your servo that specifies the minimum and
maximum pulse width, you should set the values accordingly. If your servo
comes without any datasheet you have to find the values via trial and error.

Both values have a range from 1 to 65535 (unsigned 16-bit integer). The
minimum must be smaller than the maximum.

The default values are 1000µs (1ms) and 2000µs (2ms) for minimum and
maximum pulse width.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_pulse_width
documentation:
Returns the minimum and maximum pulse width for the specified servo channel as set by
{func}`Set Pulse Width`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_degree
documentation:
Sets the minimum and maximum degree for the specified servo channel (by default
given as °/100).

This only specifies the abstract values between which the minimum and maximum
pulse width is scaled. For example: If you specify a pulse width of 1000µs
to 2000µs and a degree range of -90° to 90°, a call of {func}`Set Position`
with 0 will result in a pulse width of 1500µs
(-90° = 1000µs, 90° = 2000µs, etc.).

Possible usage:

- The datasheet of your servo specifies a range of 200° with the middle position
  at 110°. In this case you can set the minimum to -9000 and the maximum to 11000.
- You measure a range of 220° on your servo and you don't have or need a middle
  position. In this case you can set the minimum to 0 and the maximum to 22000.
- You have a linear servo with a drive length of 20cm, In this case you could
  set the minimum to 0 and the maximum to 20000. Now you can set the Position
  with {func}`Set Position` with a resolution of cm/100. Also the velocity will
  have a resolution of cm/100s and the acceleration will have a resolution of
  cm/100s².
- You don't care about units and just want the highest possible resolution. In
  this case you should set the minimum to -32767 and the maximum to 32767.
- You have a brushless motor with a maximum speed of 10000 rpm and want to
  control it with a RC brushless motor controller. In this case you can set the
  minimum to 0 and the maximum to 10000. {func}`Set Position` now controls the rpm.

Both values have a possible range from -32767 to 32767
(signed 16-bit integer). The minimum must be smaller than the maximum.

The default values are -9000 and 9000 for the minimum and maximum degree.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_degree
documentation:
Returns the minimum and maximum degree for the specified servo channel as set by
{func}`Set Degree`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_period
documentation:
Sets the period of the specified servo channel in µs.

Usually, servos are controlled with a
`PWM <https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Pulse-width_modulation>`__. Different
servos expect PWMs with different periods. Most servos run well with a
period of about 20ms.

If your servo comes with a datasheet that specifies a period, you should
set it accordingly. If you don't have a datasheet and you have no idea
what the correct period is, the default value (19.5ms) will most likely
work fine.

The minimum possible period is 1µs and the maximum is 1000000µs.

The default value is 19.5ms (19500µs).

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_period
documentation: Returns the period for the specified servo channel as set by {func}`Set Period`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_servo_current
documentation: Returns the current consumption of the specified servo channel in mA.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_servo_current_configuration
documentation: Sets the averaging duration of the current measurement for the specified servo channel in ms.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_servo_current_configuration
documentation:
Returns the servo current configuration for the specified servo channel as set
by {func}`Set Servo Current Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_input_voltage_configuration
documentation: Sets the averaging duration of the input voltage measurement for the specified servo channel in ms.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_input_voltage_configuration
documentation: Returns the input voltage configuration as set by {func}`Set Input Voltage Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_overall_current
documentation: Returns the current consumption of all servos together in mA.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_input_voltage
documentation:
Returns the input voltage in mV. The input voltage is
given via the black power input connector on the Servo Brick.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_current_calibration
documentation:
Sets an offset value (in mA) for each channel.

Note: On delivery the Servo Bricklet 2.0 is already calibrated.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_current_calibration
documentation: Returns the current calibration as set by {func}`Set Current Calibration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_position_reached_callback_configuration
documentation: Enable/Disable {cb}`Position Reached` callback.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_position_reached_callback_configuration
documentation:
Returns the callback configuration as set by
{func}`Set Position Reached Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_spitfp_error_count
documentation:
Returns the error count for the communication between Brick and Bricklet.

The errors are divided into

* ACK checksum errors,
* message checksum errors,
* framing errors and
* overflow errors.

The errors counts are for errors that occur on the Bricklet side. All
Bricks have a similar function that returns the errors on the Brick side.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_bootloader_mode
documentation:
Sets the bootloader mode and returns the status after the requested
mode change was instigated.

You can change from bootloader mode to firmware mode and vice versa. A change
from bootloader mode to firmware mode will only take place if the entry function,
device identifier and CRC are present and correct.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_bootloader_mode
documentation: Returns the current bootloader mode, see {func}`Set Bootloader Mode`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_write_firmware_pointer
documentation:
Sets the firmware pointer for {func}`Write Firmware`. The pointer has
to be increased by chunks of size 64. The data is written to flash
every 4 chunks (which equals to one page of size 256).

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.write_firmware
documentation:
Writes 64 Bytes of firmware at the position as written by
{func}`Set Write Firmware Pointer` before. The firmware is written
to flash every 4 chunks.

You can only write firmware in bootloader mode.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.set_status_led_config
documentation:
Sets the status LED configuration. By default the LED shows
communication traffic between Brick and Bricklet, it flickers once
for every 10 received data packets.

You can also turn the LED permanently on/off or show a heartbeat.

If the Bricklet is in bootloader mode, the LED is will show heartbeat by default.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_status_led_config
documentation: Returns the configuration as set by {func}`Set Status LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_chip_temperature
documentation:
Returns the temperature as measured inside the microcontroller. The
value returned is not the ambient temperature!

The temperature is only proportional to the real temperature and it has bad
accuracy. Practically it is only useful as an indicator for
temperature changes.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.reset
documentation:
Calling this function will reset the Bricklet. All configurations
will be lost.

After a reset you have to create new device objects,
calling functions on the existing ones will result in
undefined behavior!

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.write_uid
documentation:
Writes a new UID into flash. If you want to set a new UID
you have to decode the Base58 encoded UID string into an
integer first.

We recommend that you use Brick Viewer to change the UID.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.read_uid
documentation:
Returns the current UID as an integer. Encode as
Base58 to get the usual string version.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.get_identity
documentation:
Returns the UID, the UID where the Bricklet is connected to,
the position, the hardware and firmware version as well as the
device identifier.

The position can be 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', 'g' or 'h' (Bricklet Port).
A Bricklet connected to an {ref}`Isolator Bricklet <isolator_bricklet>` is always at
position 'z'.

The device identifier numbers can be found {ref}`here <device_identifier>`.
{{ device_identifier_constant }}

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.register_callback
documentation: Registers the given *function* with the given *callback_id*.

class_variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_servo_v2.BrickletServoV2.DEVICE_URL_PART
documentation: internal
Drives up to 10 RC Servos

variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.IndustrialDualACRelay
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.__init__
documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.set_value
documentation:
Sets the state of the relays, *true* means on and *false* means off.
For example: (true, false) turns relay 0 on and relay 1 off.

If you just want to set one of the relays and don't know the current state
of the other relay, you can get the state with {func}`Get Value` or you
can use {func}`Set Selected Value`.

All running monoflop timers will be aborted if this function is called.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.get_value
documentation: Returns the state of the relays, *true* means on and *false* means off.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.set_channel_led_config
documentation:
Each channel has a corresponding LED. You can turn the LED off, on or show a
heartbeat. You can also set the LED to "Channel Status". In this mode the
LED is on if the channel is high and off otherwise.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.get_channel_led_config
documentation: Returns the channel LED configuration as set by {func}`Set Channel LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.set_monoflop
documentation:
The first parameter can be 0 or 1 (relay 0 or relay 1). The second parameter
is the desired state of the relay (*true* means on and *false* means off).
The third parameter indicates the time that the relay should hold
the state.

If this function is called with the parameters (1, true, 1500):
Relay 1 will turn on and in 1.5s it will turn off again.

A monoflop can be used as a failsafe mechanism. For example: Lets assume you
have a RS485 bus and a Industrial Dual AC Relay Bricklet connected to one of the
slave stacks. You can now call this function every second, with a time parameter
of two seconds. The relay will be on all the time. If now the RS485
connection is lost, the relay will turn off in at most two seconds.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.get_monoflop
documentation:
Returns (for the given relay) the current state and the time as set by
{func}`Set Monoflop` as well as the remaining time until the state flips.

If the timer is not running currently, the remaining time will be returned
as 0.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_ac_relay.BrickletIndustrialDualACRelay.set_selected_value
documentation:
Sets the state of the selected relay, *true* means on and *false*
means off.

A running monoflop timer for the selected relay will be aborted if this function
is called.

The other relay remains untouched.

variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.SegmentDisplay4x7V2
documentation: for backward compatibility

classes.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.__init__
documentation:

the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.set_segments
documentation:
Sets the segments of the Segment Display 4x7 Bricklet 2.0 segment-by-segment.

The data is split into the four digits, two colon dots and the tick mark.

The indices of the segments in the digit and colon parameters are as follows:

```{image} /Images/Bricklets/bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2_segment_index.png
:align: center
:alt: Indices of segments
:scale: 100 %
```

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.get_segments
documentation: Returns the segment data as set by {func}`Set Segments`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.set_brightness
documentation: The brightness can be set between 0 (dark) and 7 (bright).

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.get_brightness
documentation: Returns the brightness as set by {func}`Set Brightness`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.set_numeric_value
documentation:
Sets a numeric value for each of the digits. They represent:

* -2: minus sign
* -1: blank
* 0-9: 0-9
* 10: A
* 11: b
* 12: C
* 13: d
* 14: E
* 15: F

Example: A call with [-2, -1, 4, 2] will result in a display of "- 42".

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.set_selected_segment
documentation:
Turns one specified segment on or off.

The indices of the segments are as follows:

```{image} /Images/Bricklets/bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2_selected_segment_index.png
:align: center
:alt: Indices of selected segments
:scale: 100 %
```

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.get_selected_segment
documentation: Returns the value of a single segment.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.start_counter
documentation:
Starts a counter with the *from* value that counts to the *to*
value with the each step incremented by *increment*.
*length* is the pause between each increment.

Example: If you set *from* to 0, *to* to 100, *increment* to 1 and
*length* to 1000, a counter that goes from 0 to 100 with one second
pause between each increment will be started.

Using a negative *increment* allows to count backwards.

You can stop the counter at every time by calling {func}`Set Segments`
or {func}`Set Numeric Value`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.get_counter_value
documentation:
Returns the counter value that is currently shown on the display.

If there is no counter running a 0 will be returned.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_segment_display_4x7_v2.BrickletSegmentDisplay4x7V2.get_spitfp_error_count

documentation:
Creates an object with the unique device ID *uid* and adds it to
the IP Connection *ipcon*.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_acceleration
documentation:
Returns the acceleration in x, y and z direction. The values
are given in gₙ/10000 (1gₙ = 9.80665m/s²). The range is
configured with {func}`Set Configuration`.

If you want to get the acceleration periodically, it is recommended
to use the {cb}`Acceleration` callback and set the period with
{func}`Set Acceleration Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_configuration
documentation:
Configures the data rate and full scale range.
Possible values are:

* Data rate of 0.781Hz to 25600Hz.
* Full scale range of ±2g up to ±8g.

Decreasing data rate or full scale range will also decrease the noise on
the data.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_configuration
documentation: Returns the configuration as set by {func}`Set Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_acceleration_callback_configuration
documentation:
The period is the period with which the {cb}`Acceleration`
callback is triggered periodically. A value of 0 turns the callback off.

If the `value has to change`-parameter is set to true, the callback is only
triggered after the value has changed. If the value didn't change within the
period, the callback is triggered immediately on change.

If it is set to false, the callback is continuously triggered with the period,
independent of the value.

If this callback is enabled, the {cb}`Continuous Acceleration 16 Bit` callback
and {cb}`Continuous Acceleration 8 Bit` callback will automatically be disabled.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_acceleration_callback_configuration
documentation:
Returns the callback configuration as set by
{func}`Set Acceleration Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_info_led_config
documentation:
Configures the info LED (marked as "Force" on the Bricklet) to be either turned off,
turned on, or blink in heartbeat mode.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_info_led_config
documentation: Returns the LED configuration as set by {func}`Set Info LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_continuous_acceleration_configuration
documentation:
For high throughput of acceleration data (> 1000Hz) you have to use the
{cb}`Continuous Acceleration 16 Bit` or {cb}`Continuous Acceleration 8 Bit`
callbacks.

You can enable the callback for each axis (x, y, z) individually and choose a
resolution of 8 bit or 16 bit.

If at least one of the axis is enabled and the resolution is set to 8 bit,
the {cb}`Continuous Acceleration 8 Bit` callback is activated. If at least
one of the axis is enabled and the resolution is set to 16 bit,
the {cb}`Continuous Acceleration 16 Bit` callback is activated.

The returned values are raw ADC data. If you want to put this data into
a FFT to determine the occurrences of specific frequencies we recommend
that you use the data as is. It has all of the ADC noise in it. This noise
looks like pure noise at first glance, but it might still have some frequnecy
information in it that can be utilized by the FFT.

Otherwise you have to use the following formulas that depend on the configured
resolution (8/16 bit) and the full scale range (see {func}`Set Configuration`) to calculate
the data in gₙ/10000 (same unit that is returned by {func}`Get Acceleration`):

- 16 bit, full scale 2g: acceleration = value * 625 / 1024
- 16 bit, full scale 4g: acceleration = value * 1250 / 1024
- 16 bit, full scale 8g: acceleration = value * 2500 / 1024

If a resolution of 8 bit is used, only the 8 most significant bits will be
transferred, so you can use the following formulas:

- 8 bit, full scale 2g: acceleration = value * 256 * 625 / 1024
- 8 bit, full scale 4g: acceleration = value * 256 * 1250 / 1024
- 8 bit, full scale 8g: acceleration = value * 256 * 2500 / 1024

If no axis is enabled, both callbacks are disabled. If one of the continuous
callbacks is enabled, the {cb}`Acceleration` callback is disabled.

The maximum throughput depends on the exact configuration:

```
.. csv-table::
 :header: "Number of axis enabled", "Throughput 8 bit", "Throughout 16 bit"
 :widths: 20, 20, 20

 "1", "25600Hz", "25600Hz"
 "2", "25600Hz", "15000Hz"
 "3", "20000Hz", "10000Hz"
```

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_continuous_acceleration_configuration
documentation:
Returns the continuous acceleration configuration as set by
{func}`Set Continuous Acceleration Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_filter_configuration
documentation:
Configures IIR Bypass filter mode and low pass filter roll off corner frequency.

The filter can be applied or bypassed and the corner frequency can be
half or a ninth of the output data rate.

```{image} /Images/Bricklets/bricklet_accelerometer_v2_filter.png
:align: center
:alt: Accelerometer filter
:scale: 100 %
:target: ../../_images/Bricklets/bricklet_accelerometer_v2_filter.png
```

:::{versionadded} 2.0.2$nbsp;(Plugin)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_filter_configuration
documentation:
Returns the configuration as set by {func}`Set Filter Configuration`.

:::{versionadded} 2.0.2$nbsp;(Plugin)
:::

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_spitfp_error_count
documentation:
Returns the error count for the communication between Brick and Bricklet.

The errors are divided into

* ACK checksum errors,
* message checksum errors,
* framing errors and
* overflow errors.

The errors counts are for errors that occur on the Bricklet side. All
Bricks have a similar function that returns the errors on the Brick side.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_bootloader_mode
documentation:
Sets the bootloader mode and returns the status after the requested
mode change was instigated.

You can change from bootloader mode to firmware mode and vice versa. A change
from bootloader mode to firmware mode will only take place if the entry function,
device identifier and CRC are present and correct.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_bootloader_mode
documentation: Returns the current bootloader mode, see {func}`Set Bootloader Mode`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_write_firmware_pointer
documentation:
Sets the firmware pointer for {func}`Write Firmware`. The pointer has
to be increased by chunks of size 64. The data is written to flash
every 4 chunks (which equals to one page of size 256).

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.write_firmware
documentation:
Writes 64 Bytes of firmware at the position as written by
{func}`Set Write Firmware Pointer` before. The firmware is written
to flash every 4 chunks.

You can only write firmware in bootloader mode.

This function is used by Brick Viewer during flashing. It should not be
necessary to call it in a normal user program.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.set_status_led_config
documentation:
Sets the status LED configuration. By default the LED shows
communication traffic between Brick and Bricklet, it flickers once
for every 10 received data packets.

You can also turn the LED permanently on/off or show a heartbeat.

If the Bricklet is in bootloader mode, the LED is will show heartbeat by default.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_status_led_config
documentation: Returns the configuration as set by {func}`Set Status LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_chip_temperature
documentation:
Returns the temperature as measured inside the microcontroller. The
value returned is not the ambient temperature!

The temperature is only proportional to the real temperature and it has bad
accuracy. Practically it is only useful as an indicator for
temperature changes.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.reset
documentation:
Calling this function will reset the Bricklet. All configurations
will be lost.

After a reset you have to create new device objects,
calling functions on the existing ones will result in
undefined behavior!

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.write_uid
documentation:
Writes a new UID into flash. If you want to set a new UID
you have to decode the Base58 encoded UID string into an
integer first.

We recommend that you use Brick Viewer to change the UID.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.read_uid
documentation:
Returns the current UID as an integer. Encode as
Base58 to get the usual string version.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.get_identity
documentation:
Returns the UID, the UID where the Bricklet is connected to,
the position, the hardware and firmware version as well as the
device identifier.

The position can be 'a', 'b', 'c', 'd', 'e', 'f', 'g' or 'h' (Bricklet Port).
A Bricklet connected to an {ref}`Isolator Bricklet <isolator_bricklet>` is always at
position 'z'.

The device identifier numbers can be found {ref}`here <device_identifier>`.
{{ device_identifier_constant }}

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.register_callback
documentation: Registers the given *function* with the given *callback_id*.

class_variables.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_accelerometer_v2.BrickletAccelerometerV2.DEVICE_URL_PART
documentation: internal
Measures acceleration in three axis

documentation:

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.get_current
documentation:
Returns the current of the specified channel.

It is possible to detect if an IEC 60381-1 compatible sensor is connected
and if it works probably.

If the returned current is below 4mA, there is likely no sensor connected
or the connected sensor is defective. If the returned current is over 20mA,
there might be a short circuit or the sensor is defective.

If you want to get the value periodically, it is recommended to use the
{cb}`Current` callback. You can set the callback configuration
with {func}`Set Current Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.set_current_callback_configuration
documentation:
The period is the period with which the {cb}`Current` callback is triggered
periodically. A value of 0 turns the callback off.

If the `value has to change`-parameter is set to true, the callback is only
triggered after the value has changed. If the value didn't change
within the period, the callback is triggered immediately on change.

If it is set to false, the callback is continuously triggered with the period,
independent of the value.

It is furthermore possible to constrain the callback with thresholds.

The `option`-parameter together with min/max sets a threshold for the {cb}`Current` callback.

The following options are possible:

```
.. csv-table::
 :header: "Option", "Description"
 :widths: 10, 100

 "'x'",    "Threshold is turned off"
 "'o'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is *outside* the min and max values"
 "'i'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is *inside* or equal to the min and max values"
 "'<'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is smaller than the min value (max is ignored)"
 "'>'",    "Threshold is triggered when the value is greater than the min value (max is ignored)"
```

If the option is set to 'x' (threshold turned off) the callback is triggered with the fixed period.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.get_current_callback_configuration
documentation: Returns the callback configuration as set by {func}`Set Current Callback Configuration`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.set_sample_rate
documentation:
Sets the sample rate to either 240, 60, 15 or 4 samples per second.
The resolution for the rates is 12, 14, 16 and 18 bit respectively.

```
.. csv-table::
 :header: "Value", "Description"
 :widths: 10, 100

 "0",    "240 samples per second, 12 bit resolution"
 "1",    "60 samples per second, 14 bit resolution"
 "2",    "15 samples per second, 16 bit resolution"
 "3",    "4 samples per second, 18 bit resolution"
```

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.get_sample_rate
documentation: Returns the sample rate as set by {func}`Set Sample Rate`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.set_gain
documentation:
Sets a gain between 1x and 8x. If you want to measure a very small current,
you can increase the gain to get some more resolution.

Example: If you measure 0.5mA with a gain of 8x the return value will be
4mA.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.get_gain
documentation: Returns the gain as set by {func}`Set Gain`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.set_channel_led_config
documentation:
Each channel has a corresponding LED. You can turn the LED off, on or show a
heartbeat. You can also set the LED to "Channel Status". In this mode the
LED can either be turned on with a pre-defined threshold or the intensity
of the LED can change with the measured value.

You can configure the channel status behavior with {func}`Set Channel LED Status Config`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.get_channel_led_config
documentation: Returns the channel LED configuration as set by {func}`Set Channel LED Config`

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.set_channel_led_status_config
documentation:
Sets the channel LED status config. This config is used if the channel LED is
configured as "Channel Status", see {func}`Set Channel LED Config`.

For each channel you can choose between threshold and intensity mode.

In threshold mode you can define a positive or a negative threshold.
For a positive threshold set the "min" parameter to the threshold value in nA
above which the LED should turn on and set the "max" parameter to 0. Example:
If you set a positive threshold of 10mA, the LED will turn on as soon as the
current exceeds 10mA and turn off again if it goes below 10mA.
For a negative threshold set the "max" parameter to the threshold value in nA
below which the LED should turn on and set the "min" parameter to 0. Example:
If you set a negative threshold of 10mA, the LED will turn on as soon as the
current goes below 10mA and the LED will turn off when the current exceeds 10mA.

In intensity mode you can define a range in nA that is used to scale the brightness
of the LED. Example with min=4mA and max=20mA: The LED is off at 4mA and below,
on at 20mA and above and the brightness is linearly scaled between the values
4mA and 20mA. If the min value is greater than the max value, the LED brightness
is scaled the other way around.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.get_channel_led_status_config
documentation:
Returns the channel LED status configuration as set by
{func}`Set Channel LED Status Config`.

methods.name: tinkerforge.bricklet_industrial_dual_0_20ma_v2.BrickletIndustrialDual020mAV2.set_status_led_config
